siteTitle: The Catholic Experiment | Essays, audio, and visual notes for a Catholic imagination.
siteDescription: Placeholder concept site for a Catholic journal, media studio, or parish culture project.
tagline: Essays, audio, and visual notes for a Catholic imagination.

hero.title: A placeholder home for essays, interviews, liturgy notes, and Catholic cultural work.
hero.intro: The Catholic Experiment is a concept website for a publication or studio shaped by sacramental imagination, serious reading, careful conversation, and the textures of parish life. Everything here is placeholder content intended to show tone, structure, and atmosphere rather than final launch copy.

services.title: Essays and meditations
services.summary: Long-form pieces on theology, literature, art, place, memory, and the Catholic imagination.

services.title: Conversations and interviews
services.summary: Placeholder space for recorded conversations with priests, artists, teachers, mothers, musicians, and builders of local culture.
services.anchorText: For thoughtful voices and lived witness

services.title: Liturgical notes
services.summary: Seasonal reflections, feast-day primers, and humane guidance for entering the Church's calendar without flattening it into generic content.
services.anchorText: For Advent, Lent, Easter, and ordinary time

services.title: Bookshelf and review desk
services.summary: Short reviews, reading lists, and marginalia on poetry, history, philosophy, catechesis, and neglected Catholic classics.

services.title: Parish and city diaries
services.summary: Dispatches from real places: parish halls, shrines, schools, kitchens, choirs, processions, and corners of ordinary Catholic life.
services.anchorText: For local texture rather than abstraction

services.title: Studio projects and events
services.summary: A slot for retreats, live recordings, exhibitions, newsletters, or collaborations once the real project direction is defined.
services.anchorText: For the next phase of the venture

story.title: A Catholic project should feel rooted, intelligent, and humane
story.body: The placeholder direction here is not glossy apologetics or generic ministry language. It assumes a slower, more literary, and more incarnational tone: one that can hold doctrine, beauty, humour, grief, craft, and local belonging in the same frame.
highlights: Warm editorial voice rather than sales copy
Enough structure for essays, audio, events, and visual work
Built to feel ecclesial without becoming institutional sludge
story.imageAlt: Placeholder artwork of a Catholic editorial desk

vehicle.title: An editorial rhythm for a small Catholic publication or studio
vehicle.body: This second feature block works as a placeholder for publishing rhythm: flagship essays, recurring interviews, feast-day notes, booklists, and occasional field reports. It is flexible enough for a magazine, parish culture initiative, apostolate microsite, or founder-led media project.
bullets: Room for long-form writing and shorter dispatches
Clear visual hierarchy for art, audio, and seasonal material
Easy to replace placeholder sections with the real editorial plan
vehicle.imageAlt: Placeholder artwork of chapel light and stained glass

faq.question: Is this a finished Catholic publication?
faq.answer: No. The current site is intentionally placeholder-first. It shows how tone, structure, imagery, and navigation could work before a real editorial calendar, donor model, or publishing team is in place.

faq.question: What kind of project could this become?
faq.answer: It could support a small journal, a founder-led media studio, a parish culture initiative, a review platform, or a hybrid of essays, audio, events, and newsletters.

faq.question: Does the site need a full CMS?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. This setup keeps the public site static and uses structured files for content, which is enough for a modest editorial venture that publishes in planned cycles rather than minute-by-minute updates.

faq.question: Can the placeholder artwork be replaced later?
faq.answer: Yes. The current visuals are SVG placeholders designed to establish mood. They can be replaced with photography, commissioned illustration, archive images, or event artwork once the real identity is settled.

contact.locationSummary: These are placeholder contact details for an editorial Catholic project. Replace them later with a real editor inbox, parish office, studio manager, or donor-relations contact depending on the final direction.
